questions.question: What is a matrix?
options: An ordered rectangular array of numbers or functions
A collection of diagonal elements only
A compact method of solving integration
questions.explanation: A matrix is an ordered rectangular array of numbers or functions.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The numbers or functions in a matrix are called its ________.
questions.explanation: The numbers or functions are called the elements or the entries of the matrix.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A matrix having 'm' rows and 'n' columns is called a matrix of order:
questions.explanation: A matrix having m rows and n columns is called a matrix of order m × n.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In the notation a_ij, what does 'i' represent?
questions.explanation: In a_ij, i denotes the row and j denotes the column.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A matrix which has only one column is called a ________.
questions.explanation: A matrix is said to be a column matrix if it has only one column.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A matrix is called a row matrix if it has:
questions.explanation: A matrix is said to be a row matrix if it has only one row.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A matrix in which the number of rows is equal to the number of columns is a ________.
questions.explanation: A matrix in which the number of rows is equal to the number of columns is said to be a square matrix.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If a square matrix of order 'n' has all its non-diagonal elements as zero, it is a ________.
questions.explanation: A square matrix B = [b_ij] is said to be a diagonal matrix if all its non-diagonal elements are zero.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A diagonal matrix is said to be a scalar matrix if its diagonal elements are:
questions.explanation: A diagonal matrix is said to be a scalar matrix if its diagonal elements are equal.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In an identity matrix, the diagonal elements are all equal to ________.
questions.explanation: A square matrix in which elements in the diagonal are all 1 and rest are all zero is called an identity matrix.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A matrix is said to be a zero matrix (or null matrix) if:
options: The number of rows is zero
questions.explanation: A matrix is said to be zero matrix or null matrix if all its elements are zero.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Two matrices A and B are equal if they have the same order and ________.
options: Each element of A is equal to the corresponding element of B
questions.explanation: Two matrices are equal if they are of the same order and each element of A is equal to the corresponding element of B (a_ij = b_ij).
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The sum of two matrices A and B is defined only if they are:
questions.explanation: The sum of two matrices is defined if they are of the same order.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The operation kA, where k is a scalar, is called:
questions.explanation: Multiplying every element of a matrix by a constant k is called scalar multiplication.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The negative of a matrix A is denoted by -A and is defined as:
questions.explanation: The negative of a matrix is denoted by –A. We define –A = (–1) A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Matrix addition follows the commutative law, which means:
questions.explanation: Commutative law states A + B = B + A for matrices of the same order.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If A is an m x n matrix and 0 is an m x n zero matrix, then A + 0 equals:
questions.explanation: Zero matrix is the additive identity for matrix addition, so A + 0 = A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: For any matrix A, the additive inverse is ________ such that A + (-A) = 0.
questions.explanation: The negative of A, –A, is the additive inverse of A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The product of two matrices AB is defined if the number of ________ of A is equal to the number of ________ of B.
questions.explanation: The product of two matrices A and B is defined if the number of columns of A is equal to the number of rows of B.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If A is an m x n matrix and B is an n x p matrix, the order of the product matrix AB will be:
questions.explanation: The resulting matrix of the product of m × n and n × p matrices is an m × p matrix.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Is matrix multiplication generally commutative?
options: No, AB is not always equal to BA
questions.explanation: Matrix multiplication is not commutative in general, meaning AB ≠ BA even if both are defined.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If the product of two matrices is a zero matrix, does it mean at least one of the matrices must be a zero matrix?
questions.explanation: The product of two non-zero matrices can be a zero matrix.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Matrix multiplication is associative, which means:
questions.explanation: Associative law for matrix multiplication: (AB) C = A (BC).
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The Distributive Law for matrix multiplication states:
questions.explanation: Distributive law: A(B + C) = AB + AC and (A + B)C = AC + BC.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The identity matrix for multiplication (I) satisfies which property for a square matrix A?
questions.explanation: For every square matrix A, there exists an identity matrix I of the same order such that IA = AI = A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The transpose of a matrix A, denoted by A' or A^T, is obtained by:
questions.explanation: Matrix obtained by interchanging the rows and columns of A is called the transpose of A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: What is (A')' equal to?
questions.explanation: The transpose of a transpose of a matrix is the original matrix itself: (A')' = A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Which of the following is true for the transpose of a product (AB)'?
questions.explanation: The transpose of the product of two matrices is the product of their transposes in reverse order: (AB)' = B'A'.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A square matrix A is called symmetric if:
questions.explanation: A square matrix A = [a_ij] is called symmetric if A' = A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A square matrix A is called skew-symmetric if:
questions.explanation: A square matrix A = [a_ij] is called skew-symmetric if A' = –A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: What is the value of the diagonal elements in a skew-symmetric matrix?
questions.explanation: For a skew-symmetric matrix, a_ii = –a_ii, which implies 2a_ii = 0, so all diagonal elements must be zero.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Any square matrix can be expressed as the sum of a symmetric and a ________ matrix.
questions.explanation: Any square matrix can be uniquely expressed as the sum of a symmetric and a skew-symmetric matrix.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If A is a square matrix, then A + A' is always ________.
questions.explanation: For any square matrix A, A + A' is a symmetric matrix.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If A is a square matrix, then A - A' is always ________.
questions.explanation: For any square matrix A, A – A' is a skew-symmetric matrix.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Elementary operations (transformations) of a matrix are of how many types?
questions.explanation: There are six operations (transformations) on a matrix: three due to rows and three due to columns.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an elementary row operation?
options: Multiplying a row by a non-zero constant
Adding a multiple of one row to another
Squaring all elements of a row
questions.explanation: Elementary operations include interchanging rows/columns, multiplying by non-zero constant, and adding multiples; squaring is not one of them.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Two square matrices A and B are called inverses of each other if:
questions.explanation: If A is a square matrix and there exists another square matrix B such that AB = BA = I, then B is the inverse of A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A rectangular matrix (non-square) does not possess an inverse matrix because:
options: The products AB and BA cannot be defined and equal to identity
questions.explanation: For matrix product AB and BA to be defined and equal to I, A and B must be square matrices of the same order.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If the inverse of a matrix exists, it is ________.
questions.explanation: Inverse of a square matrix, if it exists, is unique.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: What is (AB)⁻¹ equal to?
questions.explanation: The inverse of a product is the product of inverses in reverse order: (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: During the process of finding A⁻¹ using elementary row operations, if we obtain all zeros in one or more rows on the left side, then:
questions.explanation: If all zeros are obtained in a row after operations on A = IA, then A⁻¹ does not exist.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The essence of Mathematics lies in its ________. (Quote by Cantor)
questions.explanation: The chapter opening quote by Cantor is 'The essence of Mathematics lies in its freedom.'
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In a scalar matrix, if the diagonal elements are all 'k', and k = 1, the matrix becomes:
questions.explanation: An identity matrix is a special case of a scalar matrix where the equal diagonal elements are 1.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A square matrix A where a_ij = 0 for i > j is called an ________ triangular matrix.
questions.explanation: Square matrices with zeros below the diagonal (i > j) are defined as upper triangular matrices.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If A and B are symmetric matrices of the same order, then AB – BA is a ________ matrix.
questions.explanation: Calculation: (AB - BA)' = (AB)' - (BA)' = B'A' - A'B' = BA - AB = -(AB - BA). Hence it is skew-symmetric.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The number of all possible matrices of order 3 x 3 with each entry 0 or 1 is:
questions.explanation: Total entries = 3 x 3 = 9. Each can be filled in 2 ways (0 or 1). So, 2^9 = 512.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A matrix is used in computer programs called electronic ________.
questions.explanation: Matrix notation and operations are used in electronic spreadsheet programs.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If A = [a_ij] is an m x n matrix, its transpose A' has the order:
questions.explanation: Transpose interchanges rows and columns, so an m x n matrix becomes n x m.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: For any constant k, (kA)' is equal to:
questions.explanation: Scalar multiplication property of transpose: (kA)' = kA'.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The concept of matrices evolved as a compact method for solving ________.
questions.explanation: Concept of matrices resulted from attempts to obtain compact and simple methods of solving system of linear equations.
questions.subject: Mathematics
